commands.review_summary:
The PR consolidates iOS notification authorization UI state into `SettingsNotificationStatus`, updates diagnostics to consume a boolean, and renames the existing app-settings opener.

PR surface: Other +51. Total +51 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a distinct current-main runtime failure: current main already opens the app Settings URL, while source inspection clearly establishes the parallel string state being cleaned up.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining steps are ordinary maintainer review and exact-head CI gating.

target.title: fix(ios): clean up notification settings state
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace notification settings string state with typed state.
- Open the app Settings page for notification changes because the notification subpage URL is unreliable in Simulator.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: iOS notification settings action now opens the app Settings page and no longer depends on UI strings for control flow.
- Real environment tested: iPhone 17 Pro simulator, iOS 26.5, macOS/Xcode 27 beta.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: tapped Settings -> Notifications -> Open App Settings; ran focused Xcode build/test commands.
- Evidence after fix: copied live output: `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`, `** TEST SUCCEEDED **`; manual simulator result: Settings opened to the OpenClaw app page.
- Observed result after fix: notification state UI stayed coherent and the action opened app settings.
- What was not tested: physical device.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: tested on beta macOS/Xcode.

## Tests and validation

- `xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.5' -configuration Debug build`
- `xcodebuild test -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawLogicTests -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro,OS=26.5'`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Risk checklist

- [x] User-visible behavior changed
- [ ] Config, environment, or migration behavior changed
- [ ] Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed

Highest risk: iOS Settings deep-link behavior. Mitigation: use `UIApplication.openSettingsURLString`, the reliable app-settings target.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review; waiting on CI.
